product_name: Large Antique Kreamer Covered Storage Tin — 18in Canister, Lid & Bail Handle (Size 50)
attributes: Kreamer (Brooklyn, NY tinware) • LARGE tapered tinned/japanned-steel covered storage tin / canister • 18in tall × 12.5in dia • Hinged flat lid with folding swing-handle • Side wire bail handle • Size '50' (a Kreamer size/stock code, not a quart capacity) • Oval 'KREAMER' maker's mark on the bottom • c.1936–1945 • Heavy honest patina
price_status: final — $65 (2026-06-21, repriced from $45 after measured size + cross-site research); large 18in Kreamer pail is uncommon (documented Kreamers are small 1–2qt), smaller Kreamer tea canister asks $89; range $55–85, local pickup only. Thin sold comps (WorthPoint paywalled).
condition: Antique — honest as-found; heavy patina, surface rust/oxidation, scratches; complete with lid + bail handle
condition_notes: Large antique Kreamer tinned/japanned-steel covered storage tin (canister), size '50' — a substantial vessel at 18 in tall by 12.5 in diameter. Tapered cylindrical body (wider at the lidded top, tapering to a narrower base) with a hinged flat lid carrying a folding swing-handle, plus a side wire bail handle. Maker confirmed: an oval 'KREAMER' cartouche stamped into the bottom (reads in reverse from the inside because it was struck from the outside underside; faintly legible right-reading on the rusty exterior bottom), with a '50' stamp on the lid — a Kreamer size/stock number (the 50/51/53 canister-size family), NOT a quart capacity. The related Kreamer numbered line carries US design patent D102704 (applied 1936), placing this c.1936–1945 (Kreamer operated in Brooklyn, NY until ~1940). Condition is honest as-found / primitive: the dark japanned/tinned finish is worn with heavy patina, surface rust and oxidation, and scratches throughout; the interior tinned surface is scratched and lightly rusted. Lid present and seats; hinge and bail handle intact; no major dents. A handsome large antique storage tin with great farmhouse/primitive presence — sold as found. LOCAL PICKUP ONLY (Richmond, IL).

info.use: price tag — scan for item card

identity.maker_basis: oval 'KREAMER' bottom stamp (reverse-reads inside) + '50' size stamp on lid; form matches documented Kreamer numbered tin pails
identity.type: tinned/japanned-steel covered storage tin / canister (large, lidded, bail handle)
identity.size_mark_meaning: Most likely a Kreamer SIZE/STOCK number (the 50/51/53 family of size codes), NOT a 50-quart capacity — internal volume is only ~30–38 qt. Do not assert a quart capacity. (Inference from the documented 51/53 size pattern; no catalog citation, so est:true.)

estimates._note: Refine-as-we-learn. Tighten each field as evidence arrives; keep the basis + confidence with every value. est=true means inferred, not measured/proven.

circa.basis: The related Kreamer numbered line carries US design patent D102704 (applied 1936, granted Jan 1937); the '50' lid stamp is in that 50/51/53 size-number family, pointing to mid-1930s–1940s production (Kreamer operated to ~1940). No date stamp on the pail itself, so not a single year.
circa.refine_with: a patent/date stamp on the pail, or a dated Kreamer catalog mapping the size-50 pail

dimensions_in.basis: owner-measured 2026-06-21 (18 in H x 12.5 in dia)
dimensions_in.note: LARGE tin — approx volume ~2200 cu in (~30–38 qt with taper). The '50' lid stamp may be a nominal capacity/size mark; under investigation.

weight_lb.basis: typical mass of a tinned/japanned-steel covered pail of this size with lid + bail handle

refinement_log.note: Initial estimates from intake photos; maker confirmed (KREAMER + 50). Dims/weight inferred, not measured.
Owner MEASURED: 18 in H x 12.5 in dia (dims now est:false, high confidence). Large storage tin. '50' may be a capacity/size mark — comp + cross-site research pass underway.

Cross-site research (subagent): '50' = Kreamer SIZE/STOCK number (50/51/53 family), not 50 qt. Circa tightened to c.1936–1945 via related-line patent D102704 (1936). Repriced $45 -> $65 on the large-size premium (18in uncommon vs documented small 1–2qt Kreamers; smaller Kreamer tea canister asks $89). Thin sold comps.
